claim_boundary: Dry-run metadata only; no live ingestion, validation, alerting, or production readiness claimed.
guardrails: Capture metadata only at this stage.
Distinguish agency announcements from instrument-derived observations.
Do not treat press releases as peer-reviewed findings.
name: ESA / EUMETSAT
purpose: Dry-run European space/weather satellite metadata capture.

claim_boundary: Dry-run metadata only; no live ingestion, validation, alerting, or production readiness claimed.
guardrails: Preserve original Japanese and English source fields where available.
Treat early earthquake notices as preliminary until reviewed.
Cross-reference major seismic events with USGS or EMSC where possible.
name: JMA
purpose: Dry-run Japan Meteorological Agency earthquake, tsunami, and weather metadata capture.

claim_boundary: Dry-run metadata only; no live ingestion, validation, alerting, or production readiness claimed.
guardrails: Separate mission announcements from independently verified science conclusions.
Preserve original-language provenance where available.
Avoid heavy imagery processing in the dry-run layer.
name: JAXA
purpose: Dry-run Japanese space agency release and Earth-observation metadata capture.

claim_boundary: Dry-run metadata only; no live ingestion, validation, alerting, or production readiness claimed.
guardrails: Use agency-provided event identifiers.
Preserve revision history where available.
Do not infer downstream damage or causation from raw hazard records.
name: Geonet NZ
purpose: Dry-run New Zealand earthquake, volcano, and tsunami hazard metadata capture.

claim_boundary: Dry-run metadata only; no live ingestion, validation, alerting, or production readiness claimed.
guardrails: Filter low-signal events for digest use only after preserving raw provenance.
Treat magnitude and depth values as revisable.
Cross-reference significant events with USGS or EMSC.
name: Geoscience Australia

claim_boundary: Dry-run metadata only; no live ingestion, validation, alerting, or production readiness claimed.
guardrails: Use source volcano names and alert terms as written.
Distinguish weekly reports from real-time emergency notices.
Do not infer aviation or local safety impact beyond source statements.
name: Smithsonian Global Volcanism Program
purpose: Dry-run global volcano activity report metadata capture.

claim_boundary: Dry-run metadata only; no live ingestion, validation, alerting, or production readiness claimed.
guardrails: Distinguish food-security alerts from market-price claims.
Preserve report dates and source regions.
Do not infer famine or conflict causation beyond source language.
name: FAO GIEWS
purpose: Dry-run global food-security and crop-warning metadata capture.
